faqs.question: Spot Trading – Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)
What is spot trading?

blocks.value: Spot trading allows users to buy and sell cryptocurrencies at the current market price. When a trade is executed, the assets are immediately exchanged and credited to your account.

faqs.question: How do I place a spot trade?

value: Select the trading pair you want to trade (e.g., BTC/USDT).
Choose an order type: market or limit.
Enter the amount to buy or sell.
Confirm the order to execute the trade at the selected price.

faqs.question: What order types are supported?

value: Market Order: Executes immediately at the best available price.
Limit Order: Executes at your specified price or better.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Market Orders and Limit Orders?

value: Market Orders prioritize speed and execute immediately, but the final execution price may vary due to market liquidity.
Limit Orders allow you to control the execution price, but the order may remain unfilled if the market does not reach your specified price.

faqs.question: Can I cancel a spot order?

blocks.value: Yes, you can cancel an order at any time before it is fully executed.
If an order is partially filled, only the remaining unfilled portion can be canceled.

faqs.question: What happens if my order is partially filled?

blocks.value: If an order is partially filled:

value: The filled portion is completed and cannot be reversed
The remaining portion stays open until it is filled or canceled manually

faqs.question: What fees apply to spot trading?

blocks.value: Spot trades are subject to standard trading fees, which may vary by trading pair and order type (maker or taker).
Fee details are typically displayed before confirming the trade.

faqs.question: When will my assets be credited after a trade?

blocks.value: Assets are updated instantly in your trading account once the spot trade is executed.

faqs.question: Why wasn’t my order executed?

blocks.value: Orders may fail or remain unfilled due to:

value: Limit price not being reached
Insufficient account balance
Low market liquidity

faqs.question: Why was my market order executed at a different price than expected?

blocks.value: Market orders are executed at the best available prices in the order book.
During periods of high volatility or low liquidity, the final execution price may differ slightly from the last traded price due to slippage.

faqs.question: Do I need to deposit funds before placing a spot trade?

blocks.value: Yes. You must have sufficient available balance in your account before placing a spot trade.
Orders will be rejected if your balance is insufficient.

faqs.question: What is the minimum trade amount?

blocks.value: Minimum trade amounts vary depending on the trading pair and are usually displayed when placing an order.

faqs.question: What is an order book?

blocks.value: The order book shows all open buy and sell orders for a trading pair, organized by price level.
It helps traders understand market liquidity and current supply and demand.

faqs.question: Is spot trading risky?

blocks.value: Spot trading carries market risk due to price volatility.
Unlike margin or futures trading, spot trading does not use leverage, so there is no risk of liquidation.

faqs.question: Can I lose more than my investment?

blocks.value: No. In spot trading, you can only lose the amount you invest.

faqs.question: Are spot markets always open?

blocks.value: Spot markets are generally available 24/7.
However, some trading pairs may be temporarily paused, delisted, or unavailable due to maintenance, low liquidity, or risk adjustments.

faqs.question: Where can I view my trade history?

blocks.value: Executed orders and trade history can be found in your Order History or Account Statements section.

faqs.question: Do I need verification to start spot trading?

blocks.value: Some platforms require account verification to unlock full trading features and higher withdrawal limits.
Available features may vary depending on your verification status and region.

faqs.question: Can I trade spot using the mobile app?

blocks.value: Yes. Spot trading is supported on both the web platform and mobile applications, allowing you to trade anytime and anywhere.

faqs.question: How do I monitor my trading performance?

blocks.value: Most platforms provide dashboards where you can review:

value: Open orders
Completed trades
Account balances
Trading history

faqs.question: How can I get help with spot trading issues?

blocks.value: Contact customer support and provide relevant information such as:

value: Your account UID
Trading pair
Order number or screenshots

blocks.value: This helps resolve issues more efficiently.
